name: Lamb Shank Scotch Broth with Pearl Barley
description: A deeply savory halal lamb shank broth built on French searing technique, with pearl barley and root vegetables simmered low until silky and tender.
keywords: lamb shank recipe, halal french cooking, scotch broth, pearl barley soup
recipeIngredient: 450 g (1 lb) lamb shanks, about 2 medium shanks
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 medium garlic cloves, peeled and lightly crushed
1.9 litres (8 cups) low-sodium chicken or lamb stock
1 medium russet potato (about 220 g), finely diced

recipeInstructions.text: Season the lamb shanks generously with salt and pepper on all sides.

Heat vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over high heat until shimmering. Add shanks and sear without moving for 3 minutes per side until deep mahogany all over, about 8 to 10 minutes total. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion and garlic to the same pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly coloured, about 6 minutes.
Add bay leaves and return the shanks to the pot. Pour in the stock, ensuring the meat is covered by at least 2 cm. Bring to a bare simmer.
Add pearl barley, carrots, parsnip, and potato. Continue simmering partially covered for 45 to 55 minutes until barley is tender and vegetables have softened.
Remove shanks, pull meat from bones in rough chunks, and return meat to pot. Discard bones, bay leaves, and garlic.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Stir in chopped parsley and serve.
